name: Signal Arc
one_liner: Living electrical energy: branching lightning arcs of cyan signal leap between a central grandmaster node and the field, crackling on a PTP beat -- signal energy made visible as light.
visual_description: On a deep-black stage, a bright grandmaster node pulses at the center. From it, jagged branching arcs of electricity -- cyan signal energy -- leap outward toward dim receiver nodes scattered in the field, forking and re-forking like real lightning, with white-hot cores and cyan/teal coronas. The arcs are not static: they writhe and re-strike, and on a steady PTP beat the central node flashes and a sync ring of energy expands outward, every arc igniting brighter in lockstep -- the grandmaster clock synchronizing the field. A few arcs carry the essence colors (a green branch, a magenta branch, an amber branch) so it reads as separated signal energy. Fine secondary filaments fuzz off the main bolts; the whole field has a faint electric haze and an occasional micro RGB-split flicker. Heavy bloom turns the bolt cores into searing light and the coronas into atmospheric glow. It reads instantly as raw signal energy / electricity -- the most visceral, kinetic piece on the floor.
technique: Single self-contained GLSL TOP generator (no input). Branching lightning is drawn analytically: for each of N bolts, march a polyline from the center toward a target node, displacing each segment by domain-warped value noise (the jagged path), and accumulate the per-pixel minimum distance to the bolt segments into a glow field; forks spawn child segments at a few points. A sharp inner line + soft corona give the white-hot-core/cyan-halo look. A PTP grandmaster beat re-strikes the bolts (re-seeds the noise phase) and expands a sync ring. Bounded constant loops for bolts/segments. Bloom + grain finish. No feedback, no input -- drops in anywhere.

network_plan.name: glsl_arcs
network_plan.role: The whole piece. Generator (no input): branching lightning bolts from a center node, noise-displaced polylines, forks, white-hot core + cyan corona, PTP re-strike + sync ring. Generator MUST use uTDOutputInfo.res; NEVER uTD2DInfos on a no-input generator. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Three vec4 uniforms via the vec SEQUENCE (declared manually). Place nodeX 400, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom
network_plan.role: Bloom + finish. Input0 = glsl_arcs. Bright-pass + bounded wide blur added back for searing bolt glow; faint grain + occasional micro RGB-split flicker on the PTP beat. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Place nodeX 800, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: out1
network_plan.role: Manifest output_op / capture target, named out1. Viewer flagged. Place nodeX 1200, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_arcs_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_arcs. See glsl[0]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_arcs_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_arcs.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_bloom. See glsl[1]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_bloom.

network_plan.name: annotate_arcs
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over the arc generator (glsl_arcs). Mode annotate, Opacity 0.3.

params.Bodytext: One generator GLSL TOP. For each bolt, a polyline marches from the central grandmaster node toward a receiver, each segment displaced by domain-warped value noise (the jagged lightning path), with forks spawning child branches. Per pixel it accumulates the minimum distance to all segments into a glow field: a sharp white-hot core + a soft cyan corona. A PTP beat re-strikes the bolts and expands a sync ring -- the grandmaster clock synchronizing the field.

network_plan.name: annotate_finish
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over bloom + out (glsl_bloom, out1). Mode annotate.

params.Bodytext: Heavy bloom turns the bolt cores into searing light and the coronas into atmospheric glow; an occasional micro RGB-split flicker on the PTP beat adds electric authenticity. Terminates in out1.

exposed_params.name: Bolts
exposed_params.label: Bolt Count
exposed_params.drives: uArc.x -- number of lightning arcs leaping from the grandmaster node. More = a denser electric storm. Each bolt runs a constant-bounded segment loop, so cost scales with count; cap 12.

exposed_params.name: Jaggedness
exposed_params.label: Jaggedness
exposed_params.drives: uArc.y -- amplitude of the noise displacement of each bolt segment. 0 = straight rays; higher = wild jagged lightning.

exposed_params.name: Branchiness
exposed_params.label: Branchiness
exposed_params.drives: uArc.z -- how strongly forks spawn child branches off the main bolts. 0 = clean single bolts; higher = a forking lightning tree.

exposed_params.name: Restrikerate
exposed_params.label: Re-Strike Rate
exposed_params.drives: uArc.w (the strike-phase time term) -- how often the bolts re-strike with a new jagged path. 0 freezes a single strike; higher = rapid crackling.

exposed_params.name: Corewidth
exposed_params.label: Core Width
exposed_params.drives: uEnergy.x -- thickness of the white-hot bolt core. Thin = a sharp arc; thicker = a heavier energy channel.

exposed_params.name: Coronaglow
exposed_params.label: Corona Glow
exposed_params.drives: uEnergy.y -- width of the soft cyan corona around each bolt before bloom. The atmospheric energy halo.

exposed_params.name: Reach
exposed_params.label: Reach
exposed_params.drives: uEnergy.z -- how far the bolts (and the PTP sync ring) extend from the center toward the field edges. Higher = bolts that span the whole stage.

exposed_params.name: Filaments
exposed_params.label: Filaments
exposed_params.drives: uEnergy.w -- amount of fine secondary filament fuzz around the bolts. The crackling micro-detail. 0 = clean bolts.

exposed_params.name: Ptprate
exposed_params.label: PTP Beat Rate
exposed_params.drives: uClock.x -- frequency of the PTP grandmaster pulse: the node flash, the sync ring, and the RGB-split flicker all beat to this. The synchronization heartbeat.

exposed_params.name: Syncring
exposed_params.label: Sync Ring
exposed_params.drives: uClock.y -- brightness of the expanding PTP sync ring of energy. 0 hides it; higher makes the grandmaster sync pulse dramatic.

exposed_params.name: Palette
exposed_params.label: Palette Shift
exposed_params.drives: uClock.z -- nudges the bolt colors around the signal palette (cyan -> green/amber/magenta channels). 0 = cyan-dominant electric storm.

exposed_params.name: Rgbsplit
exposed_params.label: RGB Split
exposed_params.drives: uBloom.z -- strength of the micro RGB chromatic-separation flicker pulsed on the PTP beat in the finish pass. The broadcast electric jitter.

exposed_params.name: Bloom
exposed_params.label: Bloom
exposed_params.drives: uBloom.x -- intensity of the searing bloom over the bolt cores. The booth-scale wow knob; high values make the arcs read as raw light energy.

palette.ratio: ~85% deep-black stage, ~15% saturated electric energy; cyan dominant (signal energy), green/amber/magenta a few essence arcs, RGB-split only as a PTP-beat flicker event.

conference_fit: The most visceral, kinetic piece -- raw signal energy as branching lightning leaping from a grandmaster node, crackling and re-striking, synchronizing the whole field on a PTP beat. It dramatizes ST2110's PTP grandmaster timing (the central node + expanding sync ring + lockstep flash) as living electricity, on a deep-black premium stage with cyan signal energy. Reads as pure power from across the room and rewards close inspection (forks, filaments, re-strike). A high-energy anchor that pairs with the calmer fiber/scope pieces; unmistakably on-theme as 'electrical / energy / signal.'

prompt.one_liner: A self-contained electrical-energy generator: branching cyan lightning arcs leap from a central grandmaster node, displaced by noise into jagged forking bolts that re-strike and ignite in lockstep on a PTP sync ring, finished with searing bloom and a PTP-beat RGB-split flicker.
prompt.evolve: Raise Bolt Count + Branchiness for a dense forking storm; raise Jaggedness for wilder paths; raise Re-Strike Rate for rapid crackling (0 freezes a single strike). Push Corona Glow + Bloom for searing energy; raise Filaments for crackling fuzz. Lower PTP Beat Rate and raise Sync Ring for a slow dramatic grandmaster pulse. Sweep Palette Shift for green/amber/magenta essence arcs. Feed out1 into the kaleidoscope for a radial energy mandala, or composite as an energetic foreground over the packet-fabric. Drive Re-Strike / PTP from audio transients for a reactive booth loop.

build_notes:
GENERATOR CORRECTNESS: glsl_arcs has NO input -- MUST use uTDOutputInfo.res (res=(1/w,1/h,w,h)); uTD2DInfos on a no-input generator is a compile error. outputresolution MUST be 'custom' + resolutionw/h or it renders 1x1. glsl_bloom HAS an input so sTD2DInputs[0] + uTDOutputInfo.res are valid.

LOOP BOUNDS (GPU crash safety): nested constant-bounded loops -- outer bolts (MAXB=12), inner segments (SEG=14), plus the bloom tap loop (R=7). The param counts only early-out via break against the CONSTANT bounds, so no unbounded GPU loop. COST: with all caps maxed ~12*14 = 168 segDist evals per pixel plus 2 forks each, at 1280x720 -- moderate. GATE PERFORMANCE: baseline get_project_performance before, re-check after; if fps drops below ~90% of target, lower default Bolt Count or SEG. Default 7 bolts is comfortable. True generator, recomputes per frame -- acceptable at these caps.

UNIFORMS: glslTOP declares uniforms MANUALLY. vec parameter SEQUENCE (op.seq.vec.numBlocks=N, vecNname, vecNtype='vec4', vecNvaluex..w expressions), NOT op.par.vec.

COOK DEMAND: time-dependent via absTime in uArc.w (strike phase) and uClock.w (PTP). Cooks every frame only when out1 demanded; add a temp Execute DAT OUTSIDE the COMP; verify with two captures separated by a real sleep. Re-Strike Rate=0 freezes the strike phase but uClock.w still references absTime (PTP node pulse + sync ring), so the TOP keeps cooking -- correct.

DOCKED DATS: glsl_arcs + glsl_bloom each dock pixel+compute+info; HUG all six under their hosts in the SAME execute_python that creates each op, then get_network_layout. LF/space-indented shader source.

LOOK CHECK: lightning is easy to make read as mush -- judge by an actual capture (load visual-aesthetics). Verify bolts read as discrete jagged arcs with white-hot cores (not a uniform glow blob), forks are legible, and the bloom does not wash everything flat-white. Tune Core Width / Corona / Bloom defaults against a real frame before persisting. The grandmaster node + sync ring must be clearly centered and pulsing.

TDN ROUND-TRIP: all 13 params single-component (Int/Float) -> default->value seeding works on re-import (verified v6.0.26). Re-import into a throwaway COMP, confirm a couple .vals + out1 renders, destroy it. operator_count = all ops incl docked DATs (annotations not counted): ~9.
